card.title: Freelance Designer, Copywriter & Creative Professional Tax in Australia
card.excerpt: ABN, GST, PSI Rules, Deductions & When to Consider a Company

en.intro: Freelance creative professionals — designers, UX researchers, copywriters, brand strategists, videographers, photographers — are one of the fastest-growing groups of self-employed Australians. Many start freelancing while still employed, then gradually transition to full-time. By the time income is substantial, tax obligations have quietly accumulated. This guide cuts through the confusion and addresses the questions most freelance creatives actually face.

sections.title: Do I Need an ABN?
paragraphs: If you invoice clients for creative work — even just one client, even as a side job — yes. The ATO's test is your intent: if you are offering services with the aim of making profit, it is a business, not a hobby.
Without an ABN, clients are obligated to withhold 47% of your payments. That is a strong practical incentive to register, quite apart from the compliance obligation.

sections.title: What Is Taxable Income?
paragraphs: All of the following must be included in your annual tax return:
bullets: Project fees — fixed-price client work
Hourly rate billing to clients
Retainer income from ongoing client relationships
Licensing fees for creative work (stock images, templates, fonts)
Affiliate or referral income from tools you recommend
Teaching, workshops or online courses related to your creative skills
Platform income — Behance, Dribbble, Envato, Creative Market

sections.title: GST — When Must You Register?
paragraphs: Once annual business turnover (total revenue, before expenses) exceeds $75,000, you must register for GST. Freelance creatives at mid-to-senior rates often reach this threshold more quickly than they expect.
Once registered:
bullets: You add 10% GST to invoices for Australian clients
You can claim GST credits on your business expenses
You lodge BAS quarterly (or monthly if preferred)

sections.title: GST and Overseas Clients
paragraphs: Australian clients: Standard 10% GST applies to your services.
Overseas clients: Your creative services are typically GST-free as Exported Services, since they are consumed outside Australia.
However: You must still be GST registered once over the threshold, even if all your clients are overseas.
Mixed client base: Each invoice must be assessed — Australian clients attract GST, overseas clients typically do not.

sections.title: What Can You Deduct?
paragraphs: The key points are below:
bullets: Adobe Creative Cloud, Figma, Sketch, Affinity subscriptions
Stock photo, icon and font licences used for client work
Website hosting and domain names for your portfolio
Computer, tablet, drawing pad, monitors (business-use portion)
Camera, lenses, lighting for photography or video work
Professional development — design courses, UX conferences, industry events
Professional memberships — AGDA, AIGA, relevant industry bodies
Home office expenses — proportional to business use
Marketing — portfolio advertising, LinkedIn Premium, job board listings
Accounting fees and bookkeeping software
Client meeting costs — reasonable, with business purpose documented
General clothing — even if you wear it to client meetings
Home internet bill in full (only the business-use proportion is deductible)
Personal art, design courses or tools with no commercial purpose

sections.title: PSI Rules — The Single Client Problem
paragraphs: PSI (Personal Services Income) rules are particularly relevant for freelancers who work primarily with one or two clients. If:

The ATO may treat your income as personal services income, even if you operate through a company. This limits certain tax planning strategies but does not prevent you from having an ABN or company — it just means the tax rate benefit of a company may be limited.
bullets: More than 80% of your income comes from one client in a year
You work under the direction of that client
You use their equipment or premises

sections.title: PSI Warning for Freelancers
paragraphs: Having a company does not automatically mean you pay 25% tax on freelance income.
If PSI applies, profits must be attributed back to you personally in many circumstances.
The solution is often to grow your client base — multiple unrelated clients reduces PSI risk significantly.
A professional assessment of your specific situation is strongly recommended before incorporating.

sections.title: Company vs Sole Trader — Is It Worth It for Creative Freelancers?
paragraphs: The honest answer for most freelancers: Sole Trader is right until your net income consistently exceeds $80k–$100k, and you have multiple clients rather than one dominant relationship.
At higher income levels and with a diverse client base, a company provides:
bullets: 25% tax rate versus up to 47% personal marginal rate on the same profit
Ability to retain profits in the company for reinvestment (rather than drawing everything out)
Limited liability protection from client disputes and contract risks
A more professional entity for larger corporate client engagements

card.title: 澳洲自由职业设计师、文案与创意从业者税务指南
card.excerpt: ABN、GST、PSI 规则、扣税项目及何时考虑注册公司

zh.intro: 自由职业创意从业者——设计师、UX 研究员、文案、品牌策略师、视频制作人、摄影师——是澳洲增长最快的自雇群体之一。很多人在受雇期间开始兼职接单，然后逐步过渡到全职自由职业。当收入规模可观时，税务义务往往已经悄然积累。 这篇指南直击自由创意从业者实际面临的问题，不绕弯子。

sections.title: 我需要申请 ABN 吗？
paragraphs: 如果你向客户开具创意工作的发票——哪怕只有一个客户，哪怕只是副业——答案是肯定的。ATO 的判断标准是你的意图：如果你以盈利为目的提供服务，这就是一门生意，而不是个人爱好。
没有 ABN，客户有义务预扣你付款的 47%。这是一个很实际的注册动力，完全不用考虑合规义务本身。

sections.title: 哪些收入需要申报？
paragraphs: 以下所有收入必须纳入年度税表：
bullets: 项目费——固定价格客户工作
按小时计费的客户账单
持续客户关系的定期服务费
创意作品的许可授权费（图库图片、模板、字体）
推荐工具的联盟或介绍费
与创意技能相关的教学、工作坊或线上课程
平台收入——Behance、Dribbble、Envato、Creative Market

sections.title: GST — 什么时候必须注册？
paragraphs: 一旦年营业额（税前总收入）超过 $75,000，就必须注册 GST。处于中高收入段的自由创意人往往比预期更快达到这个门槛。
注册后：
bullets: 向澳洲客户开具发票时需附加 10% GST
可以申请业务费用的 GST 抵扣
按季度（或按月）申报 BAS

sections.title: GST 与海外客户
paragraphs: 澳洲客户：你的服务适用标准 10% GST。
海外客户：你的创意服务通常属于「出口服务」免除 GST，因为服务在澳洲以外被消费。
但是：一旦超过门槛，即便所有客户都在海外，仍需注册 GST。
混合客户群：每张发票必须单独评估——澳洲客户适用 GST，海外客户通常不适用。

sections.title: 可以申报哪些扣税项目？
paragraphs: 重点如下：
bullets: Adobe Creative Cloud、Figma、Sketch、Affinity 订阅
用于客户工作的图库照片、图标和字体授权
作品集网站托管和域名
电脑、平板、绘图板、显示器（业务使用比例）
摄影或视频工作用的相机、镜头、灯光
专业发展——设计课程、UX 会议、行业活动
专业会员资格——AGDA、AIGA 及相关行业机构
居家办公费用——按业务使用比例计算
营销——作品集推广、LinkedIn Premium、求职平台
会计费和记账软件
客户会面费用——合理且记录有业务目的
日常服装——即便穿着它去见客户
家庭网络账单全额（只有业务使用比例可以扣除）
没有商业目的的个人艺术、设计课程或工具

sections.title: PSI 规则 — 单一客户问题
paragraphs: PSI（个人服务收入）规则对主要服务一两个客户的自由职业者尤为相关。如果：
ATO 可能将你的收入认定为个人服务收入，即便你通过公司运营。这会限制某些税务规划策略，但并不阻止你拥有 ABN 或公司——只是意味着公司的税率优势可能受限。
bullets: 一年内超过 80% 的收入来自同一个客户
你在该客户的指导下工作
你使用他们的设备或场所

sections.title: PSI 对自由职业者的警示
paragraphs: 拥有公司并不自动意味着你的自由职业收入按 25% 缴税。
如果 PSI 适用，在很多情况下利润必须归属到你个人名下。
解决方案通常是扩大客户群——拥有多个互不相关的客户可以显著降低 PSI 风险。
在注册公司之前，强烈建议进行专业的个人情况评估。

sections.title: 公司 vs Sole Trader — 对创意自由职业者值得吗？
paragraphs: 对大多数自由职业者而言，诚实的答案是：在净收入稳定超过 $80k–$100k，且客户群多元（没有单一主导客户关系）之前，Sole Trader 是正确的选择。
在较高收入水平和多元客户群的条件下，公司能提供：
bullets: 25% 税率，对比同等利润的个人最高 47% 边际税率
利润可以留存于公司用于再投资（而不是全部提取）
面对客户纠纷和合同风险的有限责任保护
在大型企业客户业务中更专业的主体形象

takeawayItems: 我需要申请 ABN 吗？
GST — 什么时候必须注册？
